permission.title: Accessibility access required
permission.body: SmoothScroll needs Accessibility permission to capture mouse-wheel events and re-emit them with smooth animation. Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility and toggle SmoothScroll on.
permission.macos_body: Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility and toggle SmoothScroll on. This allows SmoothScroll to capture and smooth your mouse wheel events.
permission.check_now: I just granted it — check now
permission.still_denied: Still not granted. Make sure SmoothScroll is toggled ON in System Settings.
permission.polling: Watching for permission. I'll continue automatically once granted.
permission.linux_retry_hint: After running the commands above, click 'Check Again'
permission.linux_uinput_body:
SmoothScroll needs access to /dev/uinput for scroll smoothing.

Run the following commands and log out:

  sudo gpasswd -a $USER input
  sudo bash -c 'echo "KERNEL==\"uinput\", GROUP=\"input\", MODE=\"0660\", OPTIONS+=\"static_node=uinput\"" > /etc/udev/rules.d/99-smoothscroll.rules'
  sudo udevadm control --reload-rules

After logging back in, restart SmoothScroll.
permission.linux_uinput_hint: After running the commands above, log out and back in, then restart SmoothScroll.
permission.linux_wayland_subtitle: Running on Wayland - uinput access needed
permission.linux_flatpak_subtitle: SmoothScroll cannot run in a Flatpak sandbox
permission.linux_flatpak_body: Flatpak sandbox blocks access to /dev/uinput which is required for scroll interception. Please install SmoothScroll from .deb or .AppImage instead.
permission.linux_flatpak_hint: Please install SmoothScroll from .deb or .AppImage instead.
permission.platform_error: Unable to check platform status. Please restart SmoothScroll.

backup.description: Export your settings to a JSON file or restore from a backup. Useful when moving to a new machine.
backup.import_error: Could not parse backup file. Make sure it was exported from SmoothScroll.

apppicker.empty: No matches. Try a different name.

onboarding_intro.title: Welcome to SmoothScroll
onboarding_intro.body: We'll get your wheel feeling buttery smooth in under a minute. Pick a path below.
onboarding_intro.guided: Tune it for me (3 quick steps)

stats.title: Your stats (local only)
stats.description: These numbers stay on your device. Nothing is uploaded.
stats.confirm_reset: This will clear all locally-stored statistics. Continue?

battery.low_hint: Battery is low. Smooth scrolling adds a small CPU cost — consider turning it off temporarily.

scroll.label: Scroll
scroll.title: Scroll
scroll.description: Enable scrolling, tune step size, easing, direction, and look.

advanced.label: Advanced

apps.label: Apps

gamemode.title: Game mode
gamemode.description: Auto-disable smooth scrolling for known games.

stats.label: Stats

anim_time.title: Animation time
anim_time.desc: How long each scroll movement lasts

max_velocity.title: Max velocity
max_velocity.desc: Maximum scroll speed before acceleration reaches its peak

tail_ratio.title: Tail to head ratio
tail_ratio.desc: Higher values give a sharper ease-out curve

easing_toggle.title: Smooth easing
easing_toggle.desc: Apply easing curve to each scroll movement

horizontal_smoothness.title: Smooth horizontal scrolling
horizontal_smoothness.desc: Apply smoothing to tilt-wheel and Shift+wheel scrolls

horizontal_invert.title: Invert horizontal direction
horizontal_invert.desc: Flip the direction of horizontal scrolling

smooth_zoom.title: Smooth zoom
smooth_zoom.desc: Apply smoothing to Ctrl+Wheel zoom in design apps

global_hotkey.title: Global hotkey
global_hotkey.desc: Press the chosen combination anywhere to toggle smooth scrolling

hotkey_accelerator.title: Hotkey combination
hotkey_accelerator.desc: Click and press the keys you want to use

start_minimized.title: Start minimized to tray
start_minimized.desc: Hide the settings window on launch

theme.title: Theme
theme.desc: Light, dark, or follow the system

auto_disable_windows_apps.title: Auto-disable in Windows apps
auto_disable_windows_apps.desc: Pause smooth scrolling in built-in Windows apps that already have native smooth scrolling. SmoothScroll will resume automatically when you switch away.

force_enable_all.title: Force Smooth on All Apps
force_enable_all.desc: Override auto-disable for Windows native apps

reduce_motion.title: Respect system Reduce Motion
reduce_motion.desc: When the system requests reduced motion, scroll becomes instant instead of smooth

enable.title: Smooth touchpad scrolling
enable.desc: Apply easing to high-resolution touchpad input

pixel_multiplier.title: Pixel multiplier
pixel_multiplier.desc: Scale raw pixel deltas from the touchpad

excluded.empty: No apps excluded. Smooth scrolling is active everywhere.

dialog.title: Exclude an app
dialog.desc: Pick a running app or type its process name (without .exe).

profiles.description: Custom scroll settings you can assign to specific apps.
profiles.empty: No custom profiles yet. Create one to fine-tune scroll behavior per app.
profiles.delete_confirm_desc: This profile will be removed permanently. Apps assigned to it must be unassigned first.

create_dialog.title: Create profile
create_dialog.desc: Give your new profile a name. You can edit its settings next.

field.name: Profile name
field.name_desc: A friendly label shown in the app list

app_profiles.description: Choose how SmoothScroll behaves in specific apps.
app_profiles.empty: No per-app overrides. All apps use your global settings.

dialog.title: Assign profile to app
dialog.desc: Pick the profile to use, then choose which app to assign it to.

monitor_profiles.description: Assign a scroll profile to each monitor. When you move to a different monitor, SmoothScroll switches automatically.
